points.text: Crescent Biopharma — PD-1 x VEGF bispecific antibody — Clinical-stage pipeline — Lead program identified as bispecific antibody targeting PD-1 and VEGF for solid tumors; company also developing novel antibody-drug conjugates.

Compass Therapeutics reported tovecimig (DLL4 x VEGF-A bispecific antibody) met the primary endpoint of overall response rate in the Phase 2/3 COMPANION-002 study for biliary tract cancer, with secondary endpoint analyses expected in April 2026.

Compass Therapeutics expanded enrollment for CTX-8371 (PD-1 x PD-L1 bispecific antibody) to include triple-negative breast cancer, non-small cell lung cancer, and Hodgkin lymphoma cohorts following Phase 1 responses.
Compass Therapeutics received FDA clearance for the IND application of CTX-10726 (PD-1 x VEGF-A bispecific antibody), with Phase 1 enrollment scheduled to begin in Q1 2026.
Purple Biotech established a Scientific Advisory Board and initiated an AI collaboration with Converge Bio to accelerate the CAPTN-3 tri-specific antibody platform for oncology.
WuXi Biologics received South Korea MFDS GMP certification for three facilities enabling commercial manufacturing of a bispecific antibody for biliary tract cancer.
BCMA-directed bispecific antibodies have emerged as a significant treatment modality for early-relapse multiple myeloma, with new Phase 3 data reshaping the therapeutic paradigm.

Experts emphasize the critical role of BCMA-targeted bispecifics in second-line therapy for high-risk multiple myeloma patients, despite challenges in managing cytokine release syndrome (CRS) and immune effector cell-associated neurotoxicity syndrome (ICANS).
Expert panel discussion outlines mechanistic rationale for combining BCMA-directed bispecific antibodies with anti-CD38 therapy in early relapse multiple myeloma.
Clinical strategies for managing characteristic toxicities associated with BCMA-directed bispecific antibodies in multiple myeloma patients are detailed by leading oncologists.
Myeloma specialists compared next-generation CELMoDs (iberdomide, mezigdomide) against bispecific antibodies and CAR T-cell therapies for relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma treatment sequencing.

Expert consensus highlights the need to refine treatment sequencing in relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma as the field transitions toward T-cell–redirecting therapies and next-generation CELMoDs, with bispecific antibodies cited as a current standard for comparison.
Crescent Biopharma — PD-1 x VEGF bispecific antibody — Solid tumors — Clinical-stage bispecific antibody program advancing as lead asset; mechanism targets PD-1 and VEGF pathways.

Compass Therapeutics — Tovecimig — Biliary Tract Cancer — Phase 2/3 COMPANION-002 study met primary endpoint (overall response rate); 80% overall survival event threshold reached in Q1 2026; PFS/OS data expected April 2026.

Compass Therapeutics — CTX-8371 — Triple-Negative Breast Cancer / NSCLC / Hodgkin Lymphoma — Phase 1 expansion cohorts opened following robust responses in heavily pre-treated patients; Hodgkin lymphoma cohort to begin shortly.
Compass Therapeutics — CTX-10726 — Oncology (unspecified) — FDA IND clearance received; Phase 1 study enrollment planned for Q1 2026.

Purple Biotech — CAPTN-3 — Oncology (solid tumors) — Announced collaboration with Converge Bio to leverage generative AI for designing and optimizing next-generation tri-specific antibodies to address resistance mechanisms.

WuXi Biologics — Bispecific antibody manufacturing — Biliary tract cancer — South Korea MFDS GMP certification granted for Drug Substance Facility 5, Drug Product Facility 2, and Drug Product Packaging Center following five-day inspection with zero critical findings.

BCMA-directed bispecific antibodies — Multiple Myeloma — Phase 3 data presented at CancerNetwork Training Academy confirms efficacy in early relapse setting; prophylactic tocilizumab recommended to mitigate CRS severity to grade 1.

Multiple Myeloma Experts — BCMA-directed bispecific antibodies — Early relapse multiple myeloma — Discussion on combination strategies with anti-CD38 therapy to enhance T-cell redirection and activation.
Multiple Myeloma Experts — BCMA-directed bispecific antibodies — Early relapse multiple myeloma — Focus on practical strategies to mitigate adverse events such as cytokine release syndrome.

Expert panel — Bispecific antibodies vs CELMoDs — Relapsed/Refractory Multiple Myeloma — Comparative discussion of efficacy and sequencing strategies against iberdomide (CC-220-MM-01) and mezigdomide (CC-92480-MM-002) trials.

Multiple Myeloma Specialists — Bispecific Antibodies — Relapsed/Refractory Multiple Myeloma — Discussed as current standard of care for comparison against emerging CELMoDs (iberdomide, mezigdomide) and CAR T-cell therapies in treatment sequencing strategies.

Crescent Biopharma — Next-wave oncology therapies — PD-1 x VEGF bispecific antibody + ADC pipeline — Clinical-stage company; lead bispecific program targeting PD-1/VEGF for solid tumors; Nasdaq: CBIO.

Compass Therapeutics — Advancing bispecific antibody pipeline — Tovecimig (DLL4 x VEGF-A), CTX-8371 (PD-1 x PD-L1), CTX-10726 (PD-1 x VEGF-A) — $209M cash/marketable securities at year-end 2025; funding operations into 2028.

Purple Biotech — AI-driven tri-specific antibody development — CAPTN-3 platform — Established Scientific Advisory Board; partnered with Converge Bio to integrate predictive modeling for accelerated discovery timelines.

WuXi Biologics — Expansion of integrated CRDMO model for complex biologics — Bispecific antibody programs — Secured South Korea GMP certification to support commercial-scale production and supply chain efficiency in Asia-Pacific region.

Expert Consensus Panel — Treatment sequencing optimization — Bispecific antibodies and CELMoDs — Comparative analysis of T-cell redirecting therapies versus cereblon E3 ligase modulators in relapsed/refractory myeloma.

PD-1 x VEGF bispecific antibody — Mechanism: Dual blockade of PD-1 immune checkpoint and VEGF angiogenesis pathway — Phase: Clinical-stage (specific phase not stated) — Population: Solid tumors — Comparator: Not specified — Status: Lead program in pipeline; advancing as single agent or in combination.

Tovecimig — Mechanism: DLL4 x VEGF-A bispecific antibody — Phase: 2/3 — Population: Biliary tract cancer — Comparator: Not specified — Endpoints: Primary endpoint (overall response rate) met; secondary endpoints (PFS, OS) analysis pending.

CTX-8371 — Mechanism: PD-1 x PD-L1 bispecific antibody — Phase: 1 — Population: Triple-negative breast cancer, NSCLC, Hodgkin lymphoma — Comparator: Not specified — Results: Three robust responses observed in heavily pre-treated patients.

CTX-10726 — Mechanism: PD-1 x VEGF-A bispecific antibody — Phase: 1 (planned) — Population: Not specified — Comparator: Not specified — Status: FDA IND clearance received; enrollment expected Q1 2026.

CAPTN-3 — Mechanism: Tri-specific antibody targeting oncology needs with conditional activation — Phase: Preclinical/Discovery — Population: Solid tumors — Strategy: AI-driven molecule design to maximize anti-cancer potency and minimize toxicity.

Bispecific antibody — Mechanism: Dual-target binding for oncology — Phase: Commercial manufacturing readiness — Population: Biliary tract cancer — Status: GMP-certified facilities in Wuxi, China operational for drug substance and fill-finish.

BCMA-directed bispecific antibodies — Mechanism: T-cell engager targeting BCMA — Phase: 3 — Population: Early-relapse multiple myeloma (relapse within 3–4 years post-transplant) — Endpoints/Results: Unprecedented efficacy reported; toxicity management via prophylactic tocilizumab reduces CRS severity to grade 1.

BCMA-directed bispecific antibodies — Mechanism: T-cell redirection and activation against myeloma cells — Phase: Clinical discussion — Population: Early relapse multiple myeloma — Comparator: Anti-CD38 combination strategy — Endpoints: Enhanced mechanistic rationale for dual targeting.

BCMA-directed bispecific antibodies — Mechanism: Immune synapse formation — Phase: Clinical management discussion — Population: Multiple myeloma patients — Comparator: Standard supportive care — Endpoints: Mitigation of characteristic toxicities including cytokine release syndrome.

Bispecific antibodies — Mechanism: T-cell redirection — Phase: Contextual comparison — Population: Relapsed/Refractory Multiple Myeloma — Comparator: CELMoDs (iberdomide, mezigdomide) — Status: Referenced as current standard for treatment sequencing discussions.

BCMA-directed bispecific antibodies — Mechanism: T-cell redirection via BCMA targeting — Phase: Clinical use in early relapse — Population: Multiple myeloma patients — Context: Focus on managing characteristic toxicities and adverse events such as cytokine release syndrome.

Crescent Biopharma — Equity inducement awards — 35,325 options granted to 3 non-executive employees — Exercise price $13.50 (Nasdaq closing price March 26, 2026); 10-year term; 1/4 vesting at 1 year, 1/48 monthly thereafter.
Compass Therapeutics — Corporate update — Leadership changes — Bing Gong promoted to Chief Scientific Officer; Cyndi Sirard appointed CMO; Arjun Prasad appointed CCO.

Purple Biotech / Converge Bio — AI collaboration for drug discovery — CAPTN-3 tri-specific platform — Agreement leverages Converge Bio's proprietary generative AI to optimize antibody candidates and reduce discovery timelines.

WuXi Biologics — Regulatory milestone for commercial manufacturing — Bispecific antibody platform — South Korea MFDS certification enables end-to-end services for global customers targeting advanced modalities including bispecific and multispecific antibodies.
